sourceMovie.title: Mantis
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mantis (2025) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Paolo Gallina's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Call Me by Your Name
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mantis clicked for you, Call Me by Your Name (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Uptown Girls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mantis clicked for you, Uptown Girls (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mantis?
faq.answer: Try Call Me by Your Name, Uptown Girls, La Notte—each shares DNA with Mantis while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mantis?
faq.answer: Mantis (2025) is best described as drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mantis worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 0.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this film lane.
